name: Beth McElroy Kirkwood
bio: Beth McElroy Kirkwood is an incumbent Metropolitan Water Reclamation District (MWRD) Commissioner running for a six-year term in the March 17, 2026 primary. She chairs the Moraine Valley Community College Board of Trustees, where she oversees a $161 million operating budget and long-term strategic planning. McElroy Kirkwood was appointed to the MWRD Board during a vacancy and is seeking her first full elected term. Her candidacy is supported by the Orland Township Democratic Organization, with which her committee shares financial officers (per ILSBE data). She positions herself as a fiscal manager and intergovernmental collaborator emphasizing green infrastructure, PFAS remediation, and budget accountability. Her primary challenger is Sarah Bury, an environmental attorney with Sierra Club, EPA, and DOJ experience, who argues McElroy Kirkwood was a politically connected appointment rather than an environmental merit selection.
prior_office: MWRD Commissioner (appointed, incumbent); Chair, Moraine Valley Community College Board of Trustees

red_flags.label: Appointed to MWRD via political connection — opponent alleges no environmental credentials at time of appointment
red_flags.detail: Challenger Sarah Bury (environmental attorney) argues McElroy Kirkwood was appointed to the MWRD Board as a political ally of Gov. JB Pritzker rather than on environmental merit. McElroy Kirkwood's background is in fiscal management and community college governance, not environmental science or water engineering.

red_flags.label: Machine appointment allegation by primary challenger
red_flags.detail: Sarah Bury, an environmental lawyer with Sierra Club, EPA, and DOJ experience, characterizes McElroy Kirkwood as a 'machine appointee' lacking the environmental policy credentials needed for the MWRD role. This is a challenger's allegation and contested by the McElroy Kirkwood campaign.

red_flags.label: $45,000 in campaign debts and obligations as of Dec 31, 2025 quarterly report
red_flags.detail: ILSBE Committee 40660 shows $45,000 in outstanding debts and obligations as of the December 31, 2025 quarterly report, against $65,149 cash on hand. This is a factual financial disclosure, not an allegation.

red_flags.label: Shared officers with Orland Township Democratic Organization
red_flags.detail: ILSBE data shows 'Friends of Beth McElroy Kirkwood' (Committee 40660) has officers with the same name as officers of the Orland Township Democratic Organization (Committee 37462). This relationship is disclosed in ILSBE records.

key_votes.summary: Supports investment in permeable pavement, green alleys, bioswales, rain gardens, and green roofs to reduce stormwater runoff and chronic flooding, particularly in underserved communities.
Advocates for the PFAS TRUST Act, federal legislation to address PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances) contamination. MWRD has documented PFAS presence in the Chicago waterway system.

key_votes.bill: Extended Producer Responsibility for Pharmaceutical Contaminants
key_votes.summary: Advocates for legislation requiring pharmaceutical manufacturers to bear responsibility for pharmaceutical contamination entering the water supply.

key_votes.bill: Treatment Plant Modernization for Nutrient Reduction / Water Reuse
key_votes.summary: Supports capital investment in modernizing MWRD treatment facilities for nutrient removal (nitrogen/phosphorus reduction) and development of water reuse infrastructure.

policy_platform.position: Expand green infrastructure investment in communities with chronic flooding — permeable pavement, green alleys, bioswales, rain gardens, green roofs.
Modernize MWRD treatment plants for nutrient removal and water reuse projects.
PFAS remediation via PFAS TRUST Act advocacy and source control; extended producer responsibility for pharmaceutical contaminants.
Regional coordination with municipalities to reduce sewage overflows through intergovernmental collaboration.
Emphasizes fiscal responsibility and public budgeting expertise from Moraine Valley Community College Board oversight ($161M budget).
Invasive species prevention through federal and state partnerships, particularly for Asian carp and other waterway threats.

career_history.org: Metropolitan Water Reclamation District of Greater Chicago
career_history.highlight: Appointed commissioner seeking first full elected term in March 2026 primary. Board oversees $1.5B+ annual budget and manages wastewater treatment for 10.35 million people in Cook County.

career_history.years: dates not confirmed in public sources
career_history.highlight: Chairs the board overseeing a $161 million operating budget for a major suburban community college.
Background as educator cited in campaign materials.

social_pulse.summary: Low media profile relative to other MWRD candidates. Primarily known in Orland Park / southwest suburban Democratic circles. Primary opponent Sarah Bury has more press coverage in environmental media. WTTW voters guide provides the most detailed public profile.
